name: Western Union
description_short: 400 E Central Blvd in Orlando, FL hosts a Western Union location offering money transfers and check cashing.
description_long:
Western Union's location at 400 E Central Blvd in downtown Orlando, FL serves as a convenient hub for money transfers and check cashing. This standalone location operates seven days a week from 7 AM to 10 PM, making it accessible for both morning and evening visitors throughout the Orlando area.

The Orlando branch at 400 E Central Blvd offers full money transfer services domestically and internationally, along with check cashing and payment services. Call +1 407-872-7201 to confirm rates or ask about specific transfer corridors to your destination country. Staff can assist with online transfer inquiries as well.

For Orlando residents and visitors needing to send money to family or pay bills quickly, this Central Blvd location is open seven days a week from 7 AM to 10 PM—accommodating both early risers and evening visitors. Bring a valid government-issued ID, recipient details, and preferred transfer method. Western Union at this location provides reliable same-day service to Orlando's community.
pros: Massive physical network with 500,000+ agent locations globally, including major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, and City Market for convenient access
24/7 online transfer initiation through WU.com allows customers to start transfers anytime, with same-day cash pickup at agent locations
Multi-language support in 6 languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) serving diverse customer populations
Real-time transfer tracking allows customers to monitor their money from send to pickup
Western Union Rewards loyalty program provides incentives for frequent users
Accepts multiple payment methods for sending money and offers flexible delivery options including card deposits
Specialized services like inmate payments and mobile top-ups extend utility beyond standard remittances
cons: Transfer fees are typically higher than digital competitors like Wise, PayPal, or direct bank transfers, especially for international sends
Exchange rates offered may be less competitive than market rates, reducing the amount recipients receive
Website shows technical issues (Orange wallet temporarily unavailable) suggesting outdated platform maintenance
Faces ongoing regulatory and reputational challenges related to fraud, money laundering, and use in scams
Customer service primarily available during business hours at agent locations, limiting real-time support options
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who lack traditional bank accounts and need accessible cash pickup locations
International remittance senders supporting family in countries with limited banking infrastructure
Consumers prioritizing convenience and physical location accessibility over lowest possible fees
Customers in rural or underserved areas where retail agent locations provide the only nearby money transfer option
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who value accessibility and convenience over cost—particularly the unbanked, international remittance senders, and those in areas with limited alternatives. The main caveat is that their fees and exchange rates are substantially higher than modern digital alternatives, making them most economical only when convenience of physical cash pickup justifies the premium.
services: International money transfers with cash pickup at agent locations
Domestic wire transfers and money transfers within the United States
Money order issuance at agent locations
Bill payment services through online and in-store channels
Direct bank card deposit for transfer recipients
Real-time transfer tracking online and via mobile app
Western Union Rewards loyalty program with repeat-user benefits
Price estimation tool to preview fees before committing to transfer
meta_description: Orlando, FL: Western Union at 400 E Central Blvd offers money transfers and check cashing.
